steps.title: Identify the passage's focus
steps.content: The passage begins with a broad claim about people: they are drawn to sunlight, life, and human company. It then uses that trait to explain why people crowd into towns and cities while the countryside becomes more deserted. The nighttime description reinforces the same point by showing people feeling lonely in nature and longing for lit streets and voices.

steps.title: Match the purpose to the choices
steps.content: Choice A - To address common traits that motivate many people to choose to live in urban environments captures the passage's main purpose: it explains the shared feelings that draw people toward city life. Choice B - To indicate that people sometimes experience feelings of isolation in crowded areas reverses the point, since crowded areas are presented as relief from isolation. Choice C - To illustrate the idea that most people tend to prefer hillsides in the country to certain aspects of towns and cities overstates the brief daytime comfort in nature. Choice D - To caution people not to be so quick to dismiss the natural beauty that can be found in rural areas invents advice the passage never gives. The answer is A.
